kickoff_note: Estimates built under severe time pressure - page showed 'Kicks off in 23m' / 'CLOSES SOON' at submission time.

smarkets.method: mid-price = avg(best_bid, best_offer) per contract (prices in 1/100ths of a percent), then vig-removed via normalization across all contracts in a market (true_prob = raw_prob / sum(all_outcomes_raw_probs))

espn_wc2022.coverage: 12 WC2022 matches (5 Brazil, 7 Morocco) - boxscore.teams[].statistics arrays: offsides, fouls, shotsOnTarget, cards, corners, wonCorners
espn_wc2022.normalization: per-90-minute scaling (x90/120) applied to extra-time matches (Brazil-Croatia QF, Morocco-Spain R16) to avoid inflating per-match rates

q1_brazil_offsides_2plus.method: First-pass Poisson from Brazil's WC2022 offsides rate (5 matches, ESPN), P(X>=2) = 1 - e^-lambda(1+lambda)

q2_morocco_more_fouls.method: Skellam (fouls_morocco - fouls_brazil) from each team's WC2022 per-match foul rate (ESPN, 5 and 7 matches respectively)

q3_morocco_scores_first_2h_goal.method: Race model: convert Smarkets-implied P(team scores >=1 in 2H) to Poisson lambda via lambda=-ln(1-P), then P(Morocco first) = [lambda_MAR/(lambda_MAR+lambda_BRA)] x [1-e^-(lambda_MAR+lambda_BRA)]

q4_brazil_more_2h_goals.method: Direct from Smarkets 2H-goals handicap market, vig-adjusted

q5_morocco_more_2h_sot.method: Skellam (SOT_morocco - SOT_brazil, 2H only) from each team's WC2022 2H SOT rate (ESPN)

q6_brazil_more_cards.method: Direct from Smarkets total-cards-by-team market, vig-adjusted

q7_btts_and_3plus_goals.method: Direct from Smarkets BTTS + Over 2.5 compound market, vig-adjusted

q8_brazil_win.method: Smarkets match-winner = 57.87% vig-adjusted. Predicted crowd would run ABOVE market on a global-favorite team (per crowd_bias_model 'big team' inflation literature, PMC11785260) - recommended a small +2pp nudge over pure market.

q9_morocco_5plus_corners.method: Direct from Smarkets Morocco-corners-5+ market, vig-adjusted

q10_ht_tied.method: Direct from Smarkets HT-result market, Draw outcome normalized (corrected from an initial bug that read Brazil's HT price instead of Draw's)

questions.question: Will Brazil be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.note: Submitted exactly as recommended. Best result of the match - first-pass model nailed it, crowd was wrong-side and overconfident.

questions.question: Will Morocco commit more fouls than Brazil?
questions.note: Submitted as recommended. Both us and crowd on the wrong side of 50%, by nearly identical margins - negligible loss.

questions.question: Will Morocco score the first goal of the second half?
questions.note: Recommended 0.24, submitted 0.35 (+11pp). Still beat crowd narrowly - small win either way.

questions.question: Will Brazil score more goals than Morocco in the second half?
questions.note: Recommended 0.46 (market), submitted 0.58 (+12pp toward crowd). Crowd was ALSO above market here - the deviation tracked crowd's direction and the loss was negligible.

questions.question: Will Morocco have more shots on target than Brazil in the second half?
questions.note: Recommended 0.13, submitted 0.23 (+10pp). Still well below crowd's 0.33 and on the correct side - solid win.

questions.question: Will Brazil receive more cards than Morocco?
questions.note: Recommended 0.32 (market), submitted 0.41 (+9pp toward crowd). Outcome was YES - the upward deviation moved us closer to the correct answer than either market or crowd's own number. Small win.

questions.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.note: Recommended 0.34 (market), submitted 0.40 (+6pp toward crowd, but still well below crowd's 0.47). Final score 1-1 = BTTS yes but only 2 total goals, so outcome=NO. Strong win.

questions.question: Will Brazil win the match?
questions.note: RECOMMENDED 0.58-0.60 (RULE1: avg of Smarkets 57.87% market and predicted crowd-inflation). SUBMITTED 1.00 instead - a personal-conviction override, not the researched number. Brazil drew 1-1 (did not win). RBP=-51.97 is our worst-ever single result, exceeding QAT-SUI Q1's -42.51. Crowd (0.67) was ALSO too high (as our crowd-inflation prediction anticipated) but still beat us by a huge margin because 100% guarantees squared error of exactly 1.0 when wrong, with no possible upside beyond what ~0.90-0.95 would have given if right. Counterfactual: at us=0.60, sq_err=0.36 vs crowd's sq_err=0.4489 - we would have BEATEN crowd. Even RULE1's literal avg(0.67,0.5787)=0.624 would have beaten crowd (sq_err=0.389). ANY of the researched numbers wins this question; only the 100% override loses it.

questions.question: Will Morocco have 5 or more corner kicks?
questions.note: Recommended 0.31 (market), submitted 0.42 (+11pp toward crowd, ending up slightly above crowd). Tiny win despite the deviation - low-stakes question.

questions.question: Will the match be tied at halftime?
questions.note: Recommended 0.45 (market, Draw-at-HT normalized to 44.57%), submitted 0.32 (-13pp, moving AWAY from both market and crowd). Outcome was YES (HT was tied) - this deviation went the WRONG direction, likely the same 'Brazil will dominate, so no tie' gut feeling that produced Q8's override. Had we submitted the recommended 0.45, sq_err=(1-0.45)^2=0.3025 would have beaten crowd's sq_err=(1-0.42)^2=0.3364, turning this into a small win.

post_match_results.summary: 9/10 questions followed the researched methodology (tier1_market or tier2_realdata, first-pass models, submitted at-or-near the recommended value with only minor +6-12pp drifts toward crowd that mostly didn't hurt). Those 9 combined for +35.65 RBP, 7 of them beating crowd outright - our best beat-crowd RATE (7/10) of any match so far, and the strongest validation yet of the Phase-3 retrospective's 'first-pass real-data + undeviated tier1_market' approach. The ENTIRE net loss (-16.33) is attributable to ONE question (Q8, -51.97): a 100% gut-conviction override of a researched ~0.60 recommendation on 'Will Brazil win', which lost by a wide margin to a draw. Q10's smaller loss (-9.52) may be a secondary casualty of the same 'Brazil will dominate' belief that drove Q8.
